reasoning: There is currently no approved vaccine for Human parainfluenza virus (HPIV) / Croup. Vaccine development and approval takes many years, and typically candidates in Phase III trials generate significant news coverage and anticipation well before approval. A targeted search for HPIV vaccine approvals or Phase III trials in 2025 turned up no evidence of an imminent vaccine; there are only candidates in early-to-mid stage pipelines (such as Moderna's multiplex mRNA candidates). Given the absence of a near-term candidate, the probability of a legitimate vaccine being approved and added to this Wikipedia list within the next 1 to 3 months is exceedingly small. The main remaining probability mass accounts for potential Wikipedia vandalism or an error on the specific resolution dates.
